reasoning:
The historical data provided shows a steady decline in manufacturing employment throughout 2023, 2024, and 2025, dropping to 12.706 million by September 2025. Crucially, the February 2026 BLS employment report (which provides January 2026 data and the annual benchmark revisions) introduced significant downward adjustments for 2024 and 2025. Post-revision reports state that the manufacturing sector "lost an estimated 103,000 jobs" during 2025, and as of January 2026, total U.S. manufacturing employment sits at roughly 12.59 million. 

Given this massive downward revision, the baseline is roughly 110,000 jobs below the 12.7 million target. Since monthly changes in this sector are typically under 15,000 (January 2026 saw only a 5,000-job increase), a spike of +110,000 in a single month (February 2026) is practically impossible absent an extreme structural shock or reclassification. 

The community forecast of 3% is well-aligned with these revised realities. Because of the insurmountable gap to 12.7 million with only one month left, I assign the lowest allowable probability (2%).
